factors.summary: Free cash flow has improved, with net cash provided by operating activities increasing year-over-year.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $3.2 billion for the nine months ended September 30, 2022, compared with $2.1 billion provided by operating activities in the same prior year period.

factors.summary: The company is facing potential material litigation risks related to wage and hour claims, particularly in California, which could impact operational flexibility and result in significant damages or fines.

evidence.text: The Company is a defendant in multiple proceedings asserting wage and hour claims with respect to certain employees who work in, or are based in, California. The Bernstein decision may adversely affect the Company’s defenses in some or all of those proceedings and may give rise to additional litigation in these or other areas previously believed to be preempted by federal law. Application of state and local laws to the Company’s operations may conflict with federal laws—or with the laws of other states and local governments—and may subject the Company to additional requirements and restrictions. Moreover, application of these state and local laws may result in operational disruption, increased litigation risk, and negative effects on the Company’s collective bargaining agreements. Adverse litigation results in any of these cases could adversely impact the Company’s operational flexibility and result in the imposition of damages and fines, which could potentially be significant.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, such as business partnerships and co-brand credit cards.

evidence.text: The remaining increase was primarily due to revenue from business partners and improved retail spend on the Company's co-brand credit card with Chase Bank USA, N.A.

factors.summary: Leisure travel demand is growing fastest, while business travel demand is showing signs of improvement but remains below pre-pandemic levels.

evidence.text: The year-over-year increase in Passenger revenues on both a dollar and unit basis was primarily due to improvements in both leisure and business demand and bookings.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving through modernization and strategic fleet adjustments.

evidence.text: The Company expects that more than half of the MAX aircraft in its firm order book will replace a significant amount of its 431 Boeing 737-700 aircraft over the next 10 to 15 years to support the modernization of the Company's fleet, a key component of its environmental sustainability efforts.

factors.summary: Management is cautiously optimistic, with actions like extended credit policy and strategic fleet upgrades indicating confidence in future recovery.

evidence.text: The Company expects that this policy change, combined with its other attractive brand attributes, will contribute to an increase in Customer loyalty. Flight credits resulting from canceling reservations previously were valid for no longer than one year from the date of original purchase. Flight credits for non-refundable fares will be issued as long as the reservation is cancelled more than 10 minutes prior to the scheduled departure.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is focused on fleet modernization and sustainability, supported by significant investments and agreements.

evidence.text: The Company expects that more than half of the MAX aircraft in its firm order book will replace a significant amount of its 431 Boeing 737-700 aircraft over the next 10 to 15 years to support the modernization of the Company's fleet, a key component of its environmental sustainability efforts.

factors.summary: Catalysts include potential cost savings from fleet modernization and improved operational efficiency.

evidence.text: The Company expects that more than half of the MAX aircraft in its firm order book will replace a significant amount of its 431 Boeing 737-700 aircraft over the next 10 to 15 years to support the modernization of the Company's fleet, a key component of its environmental sustainability efforts.

factors.summary: Environmental sustainability efforts are a key part of the company's strategy, with investments in fuel-efficient aircraft and DEI initiatives.

evidence.text: The Company also published its first ever Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion ('DEI') Report on April 22, 2022. A companion piece to the One Report, the DEI Report takes a deeper dive into the Company's DEI goals, commitments, and initiatives and highlights the expected path forward.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is making significant investments in environmental sustainability and DEI initiatives, which are positive developments.

factors.summary: Route network changes are expected to be accretive, focusing on adding frequencies in strong customer bases.

evidence.text: During 2022, the Company has primarily focused on restoring its network, principally in cities with a very strong Customer base, by adding city pair frequencies and connecting new service with existing points-of-strength to increase Customer depth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Route network changes are expected to be accretive by focusing on strong customer bases, indicating a positive business strategy.

factors.summary: Fleet strategy is focused on modernization with new orders and retirements, positioning the company for efficiency gains.

evidence.text: The Company expects that more than half of the MAX aircraft in its firm order book will replace a significant amount of its 431 Boeing 737-700 aircraft over the next 10 to 15 years to support the modernization of the Company's fleet, a key component of its environmental sustainability efforts.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Fleet modernization is expected to bring efficiency gains, supporting both cost reduction and environmental sustainability.

factors.summary: The company is pursuing cost reduction initiatives through fleet modernization, which is expected to replace a significant portion of its current fleet over the next 10 to 15 years. However, there is no explicit mention of restructuring or other cost reduction programs in the provided text.

evidence.text: The text does not provide specific information on restructuring or cost reduction programs.

The Company expects that more than half of the MAX aircraft in its firm order book will replace a significant amount of its 431 Boeing 737-700 aircraft over the next 10 to 15 years to support the modernization of the Company's fleet, a key component of its environmental sustainability efforts.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While the company is modernizing its fleet to reduce costs, there is no explicit mention of other cost reduction programs, making the sentiment neutral.

factors.summary: Operating margins are under pressure due to rising expenses, particularly fuel and labor costs, which have outpaced revenue growth. Despite some improvements in demand and bookings contributing to higher revenues, the increases in operating expenses have negatively impacted the margin trajectory.

evidence.text: Operating expenses per ASM for the first nine months of 2022 increased by 51.3 percent, compared with the first nine months of 2021. The majority of the year-over-year unit cost increase was driven by Payroll Support received in the first nine months of 2021.

Operating expenses per ASM for third quarter 2022 increased by 45.7 percent, compared with third quarter 2021, primarily due to Payroll Support allocated in third quarter 2021 in addition to a significant increase in the Company's fuel cost per gallon.
Operating expenses per ASM increased by 45.7% for the three months ended September 30, 2022, compared to 2021, outpacing the 31.2% increase in operating revenues per ASM.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Operating margins are under pressure due to rising expenses, particularly fuel and labor costs, which negatively impact the margin trajectory.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel, are rising significantly, creating margin pressure. The company is managing these costs through operational efficiency and pricing strategies.

evidence.text: Fuel and oil expense for the first nine months of 2022 increased by $2.1 billion, or 94.2 percent, compared with the first nine months of 2021. On a per ASM basis, Fuel and oil expense for the first nine months of 2022 increased 66.0 percent.
Fuel and oil expense for third quarter 2022 increased by $760 million, or 76.8 percent, compared with third quarter 2021.
Fuel costs per gallon, including fuel tax, increased by 68.7% for the three months ended September 30, 2022, compared to 2021.

The Company expects that more than half of the MAX aircraft in its firm order book will replace a significant amount of its 431 Boeing 737-700 aircraft over the next 10 to 15 years to support the modernization of the Company's fleet, a key component of its environmental sustainability efforts.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs, especially fuel, are rising significantly, creating margin pressure despite cost management efforts.

factors.summary: Labor costs are rising significantly, driven by wage increases and staffing challenges, which are causing margin pressure. However, the company has implemented measures to manage these costs, such as published flight schedules and pilot staffing agreements, though constraints may limit operations.

evidence.text: Salaries, wages, and benefits expense for the first nine months of 2022 increased by $1.3 billion, or 22.7 percent, compared with the first nine months of 2021. On a per ASM basis, Salaries, wages, and benefits expense for the first nine months of 2022 increased 5.7 percent.
Salaries, wages, and benefits expense for third quarter 2022 increased by $200 million, or 9.4 percent, compared with third quarter 2021.
Operating expenses per ASM, excluding fuel and profitsharing, increased by 36.0% for the three months ended September 30, 2022, compared to 2021.

The Company has published its flight schedule through July 10, 2023. The Company is expected to be limited by Pilot staffing constraints for the majority of 2023; therefore, it is not expected that aircraft delivery delays would result in required changes to the Company's published flight schedules.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are rising significantly, driven by wage increases and staffing challenges, causing margin pressure.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong pricing power, supported by significant increases in yield and load factor, as well as strong demand across both leisure and business segments.

evidence.text: The year-over-year RASM increase was primarily driven by an increase in yield of 33.2 percent coupled with an increase in Load factor of 5.9 points.
Passenger revenue yield per RPM (cents) increased by 23.9% for the three months ended September 30, 2022, compared to 2021.
The year-over-year increase in Passenger revenues on both a dollar and unit basis was primarily due to improvements in both leisure and business demand and bookings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company shows strong pricing power with significant increases in yield and load factor, supported by strong demand.

factors.summary: Demand trends are improving and accelerating, driven by the easing of COVID-19 impacts and strong travel trends. Leisure travel, in particular, has shown significant recovery and growth compared to previous periods affected by the pandemic.

evidence.text: The increase in Passenger revenues on both a dollar and unit basis was primarily due to easing of negative impacts associated with the COVID-19 pandemic, which resulted in improvements in Passenger demand and bookings.
The strong travel demand that began in March 2022 continued through third quarter 2022, producing operating revenues of $6.2 billion, a Company third quarter record.
The dollar increase was primarily due to the improvement in leisure travel demand in third quarter 2022 versus the impacts to demand and bookings from the COVID-19 pandemic in third quarter 2021.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends are improving and accelerating due to easing COVID-19 impacts and strong travel recovery.

factors.summary: Fuel costs are rising, and the company's hedging program is providing some protection against price spikes, resulting in both gains and losses. Despite the hedging efforts, the overall impact on fuel costs remains significant, and the company's economic fuel cost measures reflect the effectiveness of their hedging strategies.

evidence.text: Fuel hedging premium expense (in millions) $ 65 $ 75 Fuel hedging premium expense per gallon $ 0.05 $ 0.06 Fuel hedging cash settlement gains per gallon $ 0.54 $ 0.02
The Company's multi-year fuel hedging program continues to provide insurance against spikes in energy prices and significantly offset the market price increase in jet fuel in third quarter 2022.

The Company's economic Fuel and oil expense results differ from GAAP results in that they only include the actual cash settlements from fuel hedge contracts - all reflected within Fuel and oil expense in the period of settlement.
evidence.subsection: Note Regarding Use of Non-GAAP Financial Measures

evidence.text: The Company expects that more than half of the MAX aircraft in its firm order book will replace a significant amount of its 431 Boeing 737-700 aircraft over the next 10 to 15 years to support the modernization of the Company's fleet, a key component of its environmental sustainability efforts.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Fuel costs are rising, but hedging provides some protection, balancing gains and losses without clear positive or negative trend.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by increased demand. The growth is supported by both higher demand and pricing, as well as improvements in leisure and business demand and bookings. Evidence includes a 33.2% year-over-year increase in Passenger revenues on a unit basis and a 17.5% increase in Revenue passengers carried.

evidence.text: On a unit basis, Passenger revenues increased 33.2 percent, year-over-year. The increase...primarily due to easing of negative impacts...improvements in Passenger demand and bookings.
Revenue passengers carried increased by 17.5% for the three months ended September 30, 2022, compared to 2021.
The year-over-year increase in Passenger revenues on both a dollar and unit basis was primarily due to improvements in both leisure and business demand and bookings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, driven by increased demand and improvements in both leisure and business demand.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates a strong and growing backlog of 632 aircraft orders, with a significant portion expected to replace older models, indicating a stable and expanding order book.

evidence.text: As of September 30, 2022, the Company's total firm and option order book with Boeing was 632 aircraft.
The Company expects that more than half of the MAX aircraft in its firm order book will replace a significant amount of its 431 Boeing 737-700 aircraft over the next 10 to 15 years.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong and growing backlog of 632 aircraft orders indicates stable and expanding demand.

factors.summary: The company is increasing its capital expenditures, indicating confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: The Company continues to estimate its 2022 capital spending to be approximately $4.0 billion, which assumes a total of 66 -8 aircraft deliveries in 2022.

Capital expenditures increased, year-over-year, largely due to an increase in progress and delivery payments made for current period and future aircraft deliveries during the nine months ended September 30, 2022, compared to the same prior year period.
The Company continues to estimate $900 million in non-aircraft capital spending in 2022.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capital expenditures suggest confidence in future growth and expansion.

factors.summary: Overall, the factor of capacity_load_yield shows positive trends with improving load factors and increasing yields contributing to higher revenues. The evidence points to consistent improvements in both load factor and passenger revenue yield across different subsections, though there are slight variations in the extent of improvement reported.

evidence.text: The year-over-year RASM increase was primarily driven by an increase in yield of 33.2 percent coupled with an increase in Load factor of 5.9 points.

Available seat miles (ASMs) increased by 1.3% for the three months ended September 30, 2022, compared to 2019. Load factor improved by 4.7 percentage points, and passenger revenue yield per RPM increased by 23.9% compared to 2021.
The year-over-year increase in Passenger revenues on both a dollar and unit basis was primarily due to improvements in both leisure and business demand and bookings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving load factors and increasing yields contribute to higher revenues, indicating positive trends.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by both improving demand and pricing, as well as a recovery in demand. The company has seen significant increases in operating revenues compared to pre-pandemic levels and a substantial year-over-year growth in the third quarter of 2022.

evidence.text: Operating revenues for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2022, exceeded the comparative 2019 pre-pandemic levels primarily due to higher yields.
Total operating revenues for third quarter 2022 increased by $1.5 billion, or 32.9 percent, year-over-year, to $6.2 billion.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, exceeding pre-pandemic levels with significant year-over-year increases.

factors.summary: The travel demand mix is shifting favorably towards leisure travel, which is performing strongly compared to pre-pandemic levels, while business travel continues to lag behind.

evidence.text: Leisure revenue trends were elevated compared with 2019 levels, while managed business revenues were down 28 percent compared with third quarter 2019.
The dollar increase was primarily due to the improvement in leisure travel demand in third quarter 2022 versus the impacts to demand and bookings from the COVID-19 pandemic in third quarter 2021.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Travel demand is shifting towards leisure, which is strong, but business travel lags, creating mixed signals.

factors.summary: Management anticipates continued growth despite external challenges, with cautious expectations for capacity increases in 2023 quarters, acknowledging delivery delays extending into 2024.

evidence.text: The Company continues to expect first quarter 2023 capacity to be up approximately 10 percent, compared with first quarter 2022, and currently expects second quarter 2023 capacity to be up approximately 14 percent, compared with second quarter 2022.

The Company ended third quarter 2022 with 742 aircraft, which reflects 11 -700 aircraft retirements during the quarter. While the Company remains contractually scheduled to receive 114 MAX deliveries this year, the Company continues to expect a portion of its deliveries to shift out of 2022 due to Boeing's supply chain challenges and the current status of the -7 certification, and that aircraft delivery delays are currently expected to extend into 2024.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management expects growth despite challenges, with planned capacity increases in 2023 quarters.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are showing signs of improvement due to reduced COVID-19 cases and increased vaccinations, but ongoing challenges remain from the pandemic, including potential negative impacts on demand for air travel and the company's business plans.

evidence.text: The significant recovery in travel demand, which was aided by a reduction in COVID-19 cases and hospitalizations, an increase in vaccinations, and a decline in travel-related restrictions across the United States.

the impact of fears or actual outbreaks of diseases, extreme or severe weather and natural disasters, actions of competitors (including, without limitation, pricing, scheduling, capacity, and network decisions, and consolidation and alliance activities), consumer perception, economic conditions, fears of terrorism or war, socio-demographic trends, and other factors beyond the Company's control on consumer behavior and the Company's results of operations and business decisions, plans, strategies, and results;

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Economic conditions show improvement signs but still face challenges affecting demand and business plans.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices, interest rates, and economic conditions, which could significantly impact the company's near-term results.

evidence.text: the impact of fuel price changes, fuel price volatility, volatility of commodities used by the Company for hedging jet fuel, and any changes to the Company’s fuel hedging strategies and positions on the Company's business plans and results of operations; the impact of governmental actions and governmental regulations on the Company's plans, strategies, financial results, and operations; the impact of fears or actual acts of terrorism or war, political instability, cyber-attacks, and other factors beyond the Company’s control on the Company’s plans, financial results, operations, and ability to adequately insure against risks; the Company's ability to timely and effectively implement, transition, and maintain the necessary information technology systems and infrastructure to support its operations and initiatives; the Company's dependence on third parties, in particular with respect to its fuel supply, and the impact on the Company's operations and results of operations of any third party delays or non-performance; and other factors as set forth in the Company's filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission, including the detailed factors discussed under the heading 'Risk Factors' in the Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2021 and in this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ended September 30, 2022.

The Company expects that more than half of the MAX aircraft in its firm order book will replace a significant amount of its 431 Boeing 737-700 aircraft over the next 10 to 15 years to support the modernization of the Company's fleet, a key component of its environmental sustainability efforts.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is sensitive to fuel prices, interest rates, and economic conditions, which could negatively impact near-term results.

factors.summary: The company faces multiple near-term risks, including challenges related to the COVID-19 pandemic, fuel price volatility, potential litigation, aircraft delivery delays, and certification issues. These risks could impact the company's operations, financial performance, and ability to execute its business plans.

evidence.text: any further negative developments related to the COVID-19 pandemic, including, for example, with respect to (i) the duration, spread, severity, or any recurrence of the COVID-19 pandemic or any new variant strains of the underlying virus; (ii) the effectiveness, availability, and usage of COVID-19 vaccines; (iii) the impact of government mandates, directives, orders, regulations, and other governmental actions related to COVID-19 on the Company’s business plans and its ability to retain key Employees; (iv) the extent of the impact of COVID-19 on overall demand for air travel and the Company's related business plans and decisions; and (v) the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the Company's access to capital; the impact of fuel price changes, fuel price volatility, volatility of commodities used by the Company for hedging jet fuel, and any changes to the Company’s fuel hedging strategies and positions on the Company's business plans and results of operations; the Company's ability to timely and effectively implement, transition, and maintain the necessary information technology systems and infrastructure to support its operations and initiatives; the Company's dependence on third parties, in particular with respect to its fuel supply, and the impact on the Company's operations and results of operations of any third party delays or non-performance; and other factors as set forth in the Company's filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission, including the detailed factors discussed under the heading 'Risk Factors' in the Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2021 and in this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ended September 30, 2022.

Based on continued discussions with Boeing regarding the pace of expected deliveries for the remainder of this year, the Company continues to estimate it will receive a total of 66 -8 aircraft deliveries in 2022, including 31 -8 deliveries in fourth quarter 2022, and no -7 deliveries in 2022. The Company now expects to retire 26 -700 aircraft in 2022, including 5 -700 retirements in fourth quarter 2022. As a result, the Company now expects to end the year with 768 aircraft.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Multiple near-term risks like COVID-19, fuel volatility, and delivery delays pose significant threats to operations and performance.
